scenario: A growing organisation has 50 Connector Templates across many projects -- Slack, Microsoft Teams, internal APIs, partner integrations, etc. The team lead wants to curate a central library so all teams use approved templates consistently.
question: How can a team curate a central Connector Template library?

options.text: Combine multiple practices: (1) publish approved templates at organisation scope via Web Modeler's "Publish to organization" action (requires elevated access: Project Admin / Web Modeler Admin / Org Owner); (2) version templates clearly with integer-based version field incremented before each publish; (3) document templates with purpose, parameter descriptions, and usage examples; (4) establish a governance process -- review and approve before org-publishing; (5) use Git as source-of-truth for template JSON with CI deploying via Web Modeler API; (6) tag and categorise templates for discoverability.

Without formal org-level publishing, version tagging, review workflows, and deprecation processes, the template library becomes hard to maintain at scale. Central scoping via Web Modeler's org-publish feature combined with a Git source-of-truth provides a more robust foundation for an organisation-wide library.

Without centralisation, the same Connector may have 10 slightly different template variants across teams, creating inconsistency, duplicated maintenance effort, and version drift. A central library provides a single governed source of truth for approved Connector configurations.

There is no mechanism in Camunda 8 for curating a central Connector Template library accessible to all organisation members -- this understates the template management capabilities. Web Modeler provides org-scope template publishing, Git integration for source control, and the Web Modeler API for automated deployment. These mechanisms combine to enable a fully governed, versioned, and discoverable central template library serving all teams in the organisation.

a.text: Correct. Central Connector Template library curation is a governance + tooling challenge. Comprehensive approach: 1. Organisation-scope publishing: leverage Web Modeler's org-publish feature to make approved templates org-wide visible. Project-scoped templates remain for experimentation; promote to org when ready. 2. Versioning: each template includes a version field; SemVer (major.minor.patch). Major = breaking changes; minor = backward-compatible additions; patch = fixes. 3.
